differences.title: 1. COMPILATION STRATEGY
differences.leftDesc: Svelte compiles components to imperative code at build time.
differences.rightDesc: Vue.js uses a virtual DOM and runtime compiler.

differences.title: 2. REACTIVITY
differences.leftDesc: Svelte achieves reactivity through compiler-aware assignment updates.
differences.rightDesc: Vue.js uses JavaScript Proxies for deep reactivity.

differences.title: 3. BUNDLE SIZE
differences.leftDesc: Svelte's compiled output is exceptionally small, no framework runtime.
differences.rightDesc: Vue.js has a small core, but includes runtime overhead.

differences.title: 4. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: Svelte's syntax is close to plain HTML, CSS, and JS.
differences.rightDesc: Vue.js has clear concepts but more initial setup.

differences.title: 5. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Svelte offers simple, built-in store patterns for state.
differences.rightDesc: Vue.js relies on Pinia or Vuex for complex state.

differences.title: 6. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Svelte's compile-time approach avoids virtual DOM overhead.
differences.rightDesc: Vue.js offers excellent performance with its optimized VDOM.

differences.title: 7. ECOSYSTEM & COMMUNITY
differences.leftDesc: Svelte's community is active and expanding quickly.
differences.rightDesc: Vue.js boasts a vast, established ecosystem and support.
